blocks.md: A game has state, selection, and repetition. State is the word or board. Selection decides what is visible or who has won. Repetition asks for the next move until the state is final.
**Hangman reveal.** Start with a row of `_`. Use `вҲҠ` to mark which letters have been guessed. Compress the positions and letters by that mask, then assign the guessed letters into the blank board.

blocks.code: revealвҶҗ{bвҶҗ(вүўвҚә)вҚҙ'_' вӢ„ mвҶҗвҚәвҲҠвҚө вӢ„ b[m/вҚівүўвҚә]вҶҗm/вҚә вӢ„ b} вӢ„ 'hangman' reveal 'an'

blocks.prompt: Define `reveal`, which shows guessed letters and `_` for the rest. It will be tested as `'apple' reveal 'pe'` and should give `_pp_e`.
blocks.solution: revealвҶҗ{bвҶҗ(вүўвҚә)вҚҙ'_' вӢ„ mвҶҗвҚәвҲҠвҚө вӢ„ b[m/вҚівүўвҚә]вҶҗm/вҚә вӢ„ b}
hints: Start with blanks shaped like the word.
blocks.explain: The mask marks visible letters and their positions. Compressing both the positions and letters by the same mask lets assignment reveal only guessed letters.

blocks.prompt: Define `wrong`, which counts how many guessed letters `вҚө` are not in word `вҚә`. It will be tested as `'apple' wrong 'xpz'` and should give 2.
hints: Start by testing guessed letters against the word.
blocks.explain: вҚөвҲҠвҚә marks guessed letters that are in the word. Negating that mask and summing counts only wrong guesses.

blocks.md: **Hangman play.** The board below uses the same `reveal` definition. Each guess changes the guessed-letter set, then `reveal` draws the visible word.

blocks.label: Hangman: guess the hidden word, six lives.

blocks.md: **Tic-tac-toe board.** The board is a 3 by 3 character matrix of `.`. A move is indexed assignment into one cell.

blocks.code: bвҶҗ3 3вҚҙ'.' вӢ„ b[1;1]вҶҗ'X' вӢ„ b[2;2]вҶҗ'O' вӢ„ b[1;3]вҶҗ'X' вӢ„ b

blocks.md: **Tic-tac-toe win check.** Number the cells 1 through 9. There are eight winning lines: three rows, three columns, two diagonals. Store them as an 8 by 3 matrix of positions named `L`.
Now make a mask with `вҚө='X'`. Index that mask by `L` to gather the three cells of each line. `вҲ§/` tests each line. `вҲЁ/` tests whether any line won.

blocks.code: winsвҶҗ{mвҶҗвҚө='X' вӢ„ LвҶҗ8 3вҚҙ1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9 1 4 7 2 5 8 3 6 9 1 5 9 3 5 7 вӢ„ вҲЁ/вҲ§/m[L]} вӢ„ wins 'XXX...OO.'
winsвҶҗ{mвҶҗвҚө='X' вӢ„ LвҶҗ8 3вҚҙ1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9 1 4 7 2 5 8 3 6 9 1 5 9 3 5 7 вӢ„ вҲЁ/вҲ§/m[L]} вӢ„ wins 'X....X.X.'

blocks.prompt: Build a tic-tac-toe board: a 3 by 3 matrix of `.`. Place `X` at the top left and `O` in the centre, then show the board.
blocks.solution: bвҶҗ3 3вҚҙ'.' вӢ„ b[1;1]вҶҗ'X' вӢ„ b[2;2]вҶҗ'O' вӢ„ b
hints: Make b as a 3 by 3 character matrix.
Use b[row;column]вҶҗ for the two moves, then show b.
blocks.explain: The board is just a character matrix. Indexed assignment changes the selected cells and leaves the remaining dots untouched.

blocks.prompt: Define `wins`, which reports whether `X` has a complete line on a 9-character board. It will be tested on `'XXX...OO.'` and should give 1.
hints: Make an X mask from the board.
Gather each winning line by indexing the mask with L.
Use вҲ§/ for each line and вҲЁ/ across the line results.
blocks.explain: m[L] turns the eight winning triples into rows of Boolean values. вҲ§/ finds complete rows, and вҲЁ/ asks whether any complete row exists.
